flows.title: reset-60s
steps: Sit down. Hands rested on thighs, not gripping.
Physiological sigh times four. Double inhale through nose, long exhale through mouth.
Name three things you feel. Fabric, breath, seat.
Say: My nervous system is doing a lot. I am being gentle with it.
Sip water.
success: You can name what you were doing before

flows.title: reset-5min
steps: Minute one. Settle. Sit or recline. Hands palm-down. Eyes soft.
Minute two. Breath. Four in, six out. Exhales longer than inhales.
Minute three. Body scan. Head to feet. Notice without changing.
Minute four. One small care. Water, temperature, blanket, or dim light.
Minute five. Reentry. Say your choice out loud: continue, switch, or stop.

flows.title: reset-deep
flows.trigger: Severity 8 to 10 or two failed reset-5min attempts
steps: Body horizontal. Knees supported if helpful. Hands on belly.
Long exhale breath. Four in, eight out. Five minutes minimum.
Temperature regulation. Blanket, socks, or cool cloth on forehead.
Weighted blanket or compression if it feels good. Remove if worse.
No content. Sound only or silence.
Stay at least twenty minutes, even if you feel better at ten.
Reentry. Sleep, rest, or one low-load activity. No work.

flows.title: shutdown
steps: Close open loops. Save work. Close the laptop.
Phone on airplane mode or Do Not Disturb. Charging across the room.
Water by the bed. Lukewarm.
Teeth. Even a quick brush.
One sentence out loud about today. Not an evaluation, just a fact.
One sentence about tomorrow's first move. A single step only.
Lights off.
Reset-deep breathing. Four in, eight out. Five minutes while horizontal.

flows.title: Hijacked
steps: Say out loud: My nervous system is firing. This is not danger. This is a wave.
Sit or lie down. Standing extends the spike.
Cool-ish water on wrists or face for twenty seconds. Not ice.
Physiological sigh times six.
Name what's around you. Five see, four touch, three hear. Out loud.
No decisions. No texts, posts, or commits.
success: Can name the day of the week

flows.title: Pain Spike
flows.trigger: Sudden burning, stabbing, or electrical pain
steps: Posture check. Standing? Sit. Sitting tense? Recline.
Temperature match. Cool if hot. Lukewarm if icy. Never extreme.
Light compression test. Soft sock or weighted blanket. Remove if worse.
Breath. Four in, seven out.
Visualize the area as warm and neutral, not as the pain location.
Tiny movement. Ankle circles, finger fan, or slow jaw opens.
success: Pain drops at least 1 point in 10 minutes

flows.title: Itch Flare
steps: Remove contact source. Wash cool then warm soapy water.
Do not scratch. Press firmly with a flat palm.
Cool damp cloth for sixty to one-twenty seconds.
If SFN-likely: phantom itch, nerves are noisy. Do not engage.
Distraction. Different sensory channel.
Unscented lotion if skin tolerates.

flows.title: Sensory Overload
steps: Reduce one input. Close eyes, cover ears, or step out.
Find low-load space.
Earplugs, hood, or sunglasses.
Sit. Eyes soft.
One breath. Four in, eight out.
Five minutes minimum before reentering.

flows.title: Doom Loop
steps: Name it: I am in a doom loop. The thoughts are real but not accurate right now.
Body check first. When did you last eat, drink, sleep, pee?
Take care of one body need.
Change the input channel. Different room, background sound, open window.
Do not problem-solve the thoughts. Wait them out from a regulated body.
Talk to a human if safe, to be witnessed.
Nine eight eight if anything tips toward not wanting to be here.
success: Thoughts soften from absolute to relative
